title: Device API & wiring
lead: This page documents how IoT devices integrate with Flovers. Devices can send environmental readings to the backend, check whether a manual watering job has been scheduled from the mobile app, and report completed pump runs back to Flovers. The generated Arduino/ESP firmware can support both readings and watering control in one sketch.

overview.title: What the generated firmware does
overview.intro: The generated firmware can connect to Wi-Fi, synchronize time using NTP, read enabled sensors, send readings to Flovers, check for scheduled manual watering jobs, run the pump when a manual watering job is pending, check backend-controlled automatic watering settings, run automatic watering when soil moisture is below the configured threshold, and report watering completion back to the backend.
overview.defaultBehavior: By default, readings are sent once per hour. Manual watering jobs are checked more frequently, for example once per minute, so a watering request created from the mobile app can be picked up without waiting for the next hourly reading cycle.
overview.adjustmentNote: Some values should be adjusted for your hardware and preferences, especially Wi-Fi credentials, pin numbers, soil moisture calibration, pump runtime, reading interval, manual job polling interval, and automatic watering cooldown.

endpoints.title: API endpoints
endpoints.note: Devices send telemetry to the ingest endpoint, can check for pending pump jobs, can report completed watering, and can read the latest device feed for diagnostics or simple integrations.

ingest.label: Ingest readings (device → backend)
ingest.desc: Devices use this endpoint to send sensor readings. The backend authenticates the request using secret, device_key, and optionally device_id.

pumpNextTask.label: Check next pump task (device → backend)
pumpNextTask.desc: Devices with pump support use this endpoint to check whether a manual watering job is waiting. The response also includes the current backend-controlled automatic pump configuration.

pumpComplete.label: Report pump completion (device → backend)
pumpComplete.desc: Devices use this endpoint after the pump has run. This can report either a scheduled manual watering task or an automatic watering event.

feed.label: Read latest device feed (device → backend)
feed.desc: This endpoint returns the latest reading data for a device. It is still useful for simple integrations or diagnostics, but pump-enabled generated firmware uses pump-next-task and pump-complete for watering control.

ingest.title: POST ingest documentation
ingest.desc: Send one reading sample as JSON. The backend authenticates the device and stores the provided metrics under the matching device.
ingest.psCode:
# PowerShell example: send one reading

$body = @'
{
  "secret": "YOUR_SECRET",
  "device_key": "YOUR_DEVICE_KEY",
  "device_id": 12,
  "timestamp": "2026-01-06T10:11:00Z",
  "metrics": {
    "temperature": 20.5,
    "humidity": 40.0,
    "light": 520,
    "moisture": 40
  }
}
'@

Invoke-RestMethod `
  -Uri "https://api.flovers.app/api/readings/ingest/" `
  -Method POST `
  -ContentType "application/json" `
  -Body $body

pumpNextTask.title: POST pump-next-task documentation
pumpNextTask.desc: This endpoint is used by devices with pump support. The device asks the backend whether a scheduled manual watering job exists. The backend returns whether the pump should run now, the task ID if a manual watering task exists, and the current automatic pump settings.
pumpNextTask.responseNoTaskLabel: Example response with no manual watering job
pumpNextTask.responseTaskLabel: Example response with a manual watering job
pumpNextTask.responseNoTaskCode:
{
  "run": false,
  "task_id": null,
  "source": null,
  "reason": null,
  "pump_included": true,
  "auto_pump_enabled": true,
  "auto_pump_threshold_pct": 30,
  "moisture_sensor_enabled": true
}
pumpNextTask.psCode:
# PowerShell example: check for pending manual watering task

$body = @'
{
  "secret": "YOUR_SECRET",
  "device_key": "YOUR_DEVICE_KEY",
  "device_id": 12
}
'@

Invoke-RestMethod `
  -Uri "https://api.flovers.app/api/readings/pump-next-task/" `
  -Method POST `
  -ContentType "application/json" `
  -Body $body

pumpComplete.title: POST pump-complete documentation
pumpComplete.desc: After the pump runs, the device reports completion. For manual watering, include the task_id returned by pump-next-task. For automatic watering, omit task_id and use source: "automatic".
pumpComplete.psCode:
# PowerShell example: report manual watering completion

$body = @'
{
  "secret": "YOUR_SECRET",
  "device_key": "YOUR_DEVICE_KEY",
  "device_id": 12,
  "task_id": 123,
  "source": "manual",
  "success": true
}
'@

Invoke-RestMethod `
  -Uri "https://api.flovers.app/api/readings/pump-complete/" `
  -Method POST `
  -ContentType "application/json" `
  -Body $body

feed.title: GET feed documentation
feed.desc: The feed endpoint returns the latest reading for a device. It can be used for diagnostics or simple integrations that only need to read the latest stored values.

firmware.title: Generated Arduino / ESP32 firmware
firmware.desc: The app can generate firmware for the selected device. The generated firmware includes the correct API endpoints, auth secret, device key, device ID, enabled sensors, and pump settings for that device.
firmware.pumpDesc: If the device is configured with a water pump, the generated code includes both readings and water pump control. It checks manual watering jobs separately from the hourly reading cycle, and automatic watering uses the backend-controlled threshold and toggle.
firmware.adjustBeforeUpload: Before uploading the sketch, adjust the values for your hardware.
firmware.codeLabel: Generated Arduino firmware: readings + water pump control
firmware.codeNote: The generated code shown here is an example. Your app-generated code will contain the selected device ID, device key, auth secret, enabled sensors, and pump configuration.

behavior.title: Firmware behavior summary
behavior.fullCycleDesc: Every SEND_INTERVAL_MS, the board runs a full cycle: reads sensors, sends readings to the ingest endpoint, checks for scheduled manual watering, runs manual watering if a task is pending, otherwise checks automatic watering conditions, and runs automatic watering if backend settings allow it and soil moisture is below threshold.
behavior.manualCheckDesc: Every MANUAL_PUMP_CHECK_INTERVAL_MS, the board checks only for scheduled manual watering jobs. This shorter check does not send readings and does not run automatic watering. It only runs a pending manual watering job if one exists.
behavior.manualCheckBenefit: This allows the app’s Schedule watering action to be picked up quickly without forcing sensor readings to be sent every minute.
behavior.automaticDesc: Automatic watering is controlled by the backend settings configured in the mobile app.

automaticConditions.belowThreshold: The latest measured soil moisture is below the backend threshold
automaticConditions.cooldownPassed: The automatic watering cooldown has passed

behavior.cooldownDesc: After either manual watering or automatic watering runs, the firmware starts the automatic watering cooldown. This helps avoid a manual watering job and automatic watering happening too close together.
behavior.manualDesc: Manual watering is scheduled from the mobile app. Manual watering intentionally ignores current soil moisture, automatic watering threshold, and automatic watering cooldown. This means that when the user manually schedules watering, the board treats it as an explicit request to run the pump once.

hardware.title: Hardware notes
hardware.desc: The generated firmware assumes common ESP32-compatible components.

components.bh1750: BH1750 / GY-302 light sensor over I2C
components.bme280: BME280 temperature and humidity sensor over I2C
components.moisture: Capacitive soil moisture sensor on an analog pin
components.pump: Relay or pump driver connected to a digital output pin

hardware.warning: Verify all wiring before powering the pump.
hardware.relayNote: Some relay modules are active LOW, while others are active HIGH. If your relay works in reverse, you may need to swap the HIGH and LOW values used when running the pump.

calibration.title: Calibration notes
calibration.desc: Soil moisture sensors must be calibrated for reliable results.
calibration.note: These values are examples only. Measure your sensor value in dry soil or air, then measure it again in wet soil or water, and update these constants accordingly.

safety.title: Safety notes
safety.desc: The generated firmware is a starting point. Before leaving a pump unattended, test the pump manually, confirm the relay logic, verify that the pump turns off correctly, use a safe pump runtime, confirm that soil moisture values are realistic, protect electronics from water, and use a suitable power supply and isolation for the pump.
safety.responsibility: Flovers stores readings and watering events, but device wiring, calibration, pump behavior, and physical installation remain the user’s responsibility.

wiring.desc: Below is a sample device wiring schema for sending readings and optionally controlling a water pump. Click the image to open it in full screen.
